_comment: Pterodactyl-compatible export of the Palworld (Official Docker Image) egg. Importable into Pelican and Pterodactyl.
name: Palworld (SoulHosting) v12
author: pascal.hannemann@gmail.com
description:
Palworld dedicated server installed from the OFFICIAL Docker image published by Pocketpair
(ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver). The install script fetches the official image's layers from
the GHCR registry and extracts the server files into the server volume; to update the server,
updates happen automatically at startup while the "Auto Update" variable is enabled (digest
check against the official image, version selectable via the Image Tag variable) - the
panel's "Reinstall" button always force-refreshes. World data (Pal/Saved) is preserved
either way. Server settings are managed through panel variables and
Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini.
startup: if [ "${AUTO_UPDATE}" == "1" ] && [ -f ./update.sh ]; then bash ./update.sh || echo "[update] FAILED - starting the currently installed version"; fi; ./PalworldServerConfigParser; (while read cmd; do rcon -s -a "localhost:$RCON_PORT" -p "$ADMIN_PASSWORD" "$cmd";done) < /dev/stdin & /home/container/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping Pal $(if [ "${COMMUNITY_SERVER}" == "1" ]; then echo "-publiclobby"; fi) $(if [ "${PERF_THREADS}" == "1" ]; then echo "-useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS"; fi) -port={{SERVER_PORT}} -publicport={{SERVER_PORT}} -servername="{{SERVER_NAME}}" -players={{MAX_PLAYERS}} $(if [ -n "$SERVER_PASSWORD" ]; then echo "-serverpassword=\"${SERVER_PASSWORD}\""; fi) -adminpassword="{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}" -rcon

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Palworld installation from the OFFICIAL dedicated server Docker image
# (ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver) - WITHOUT running that image directly:
# it declares ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh","/pal/Package/PalServer.sh"] and Wings
# passes the install entrypoint as docker Cmd, so using it as the install
# container would boot the game instead of this script. Instead, the
# image's layers are pulled straight from the GHCR registry and the
# server files (/pal/Package) are extracted from them.
#
# Install container: ghcr.io/parkervcp/installers:debian (curl/jq/tar included)
# Server Files:      /mnt/server
#
# UPDATING: with the "Auto Update" variable on (default), the server
# checks the official image's digest at every startup and re-fetches the
# files when Pocketpair published a new version. The panel's "Reinstall"
# button remains the force-refresh hammer. World data (Pal/Saved) is
# preserved either way.
#
# The fetch/extract logic lives in update.sh, which this script writes
# into the server dir and then runs with FORCE=1 - install, reinstall
# and auto-update share the exact same code path.

set -euo pipefail

cd /mnt/server

# update.sh needs jq at boot; the runtime image has none, so bundle a
# static build next to the server files.
echo "Fetching a static jq for update.sh ..."
mkdir -p .tools
curl -fsSL --retry 5 -o .tools/jq.tmp https://github.com/jqlang/jq/releases/latest/download/jq-linux-amd64
mv .tools/jq.tmp .tools/jq
chmod +x .tools/jq

cat > update.sh <<'UPDATE_EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Palworld server files from the OFFICIAL Docker image
# (ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver). Written by the egg install script;
# run by it with FORCE=1 (always downloads) and by the server startup
# when AUTO_UPDATE=1 (digest check first - a boot with no upstream
# change costs about a second). World data (Pal/Saved) is moved aside
# during extraction and restored by an EXIT trap even if a download
# dies halfway.

set -euo pipefail

DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "${DIR}"
JQ="${DIR}/.tools/jq"
REGISTRY=ghcr.io
REPO=pocketpairjp/palserver
TAG="${OFFICIAL_IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
ACCEPT='application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json'
DIGEST_FILE="${DIR}/.official-image.digest"

TOKEN=$(curl -fsSL "https://${REGISTRY}/token?scope=repository:${REPO}:pull" | "${JQ}" -r .token)

# The registry returns the tag's digest as a response header - that is
# the cheap freshness probe.
REMOTE_DIGEST=$(curl -fsSLI -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" -H "Accept: ${ACCEPT}" \
    "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/manifests/${TAG}" | tr -d '\r' | awk 'tolower($1) == "docker-content-digest:" {print $2}')
[ -n "${REMOTE_DIGEST}" ] || { echo "[update] could not resolve digest for image tag '${TAG}'"; exit 1; }

if [ "${FORCE:-0}" != "1" ] && [ -f "${DIGEST_FILE}" ] && [ "$(cat "${DIGEST_FILE}")" == "${REMOTE_DIGEST}" ]; then
    echo "[update] server files are up to date (image '${TAG}')"
    exit 0
fi

echo "[update] fetching server files from image '${TAG}' ..."
MANIFEST=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" -H "Accept: ${ACCEPT}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/manifests/${TAG}")

# Multi-arch index -> pick the linux/amd64 manifest
if "${JQ}" -e '.manifests' > /dev/null <<< "${MANIFEST}"; then
    PLATFORM_DIGEST=$("${JQ}" -r '[.manifests[] | select(.platform.os == "linux" and .platform.architecture == "amd64")][0].digest' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
    MANIFEST=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" -H "Accept: ${ACCEPT}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/manifests/${PLATFORM_DIGEST}")
fi

LAYERS=$("${JQ}" -r '.layers[].digest' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
TOTAL_MB=$("${JQ}" -r '[.layers[].size] | add / 1048576 | floor' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
echo "[update] $(wc -l <<< "${LAYERS}") layers, ~${TOTAL_MB} MB to download"

# Preserve world data + config during extraction (kept on the same
# mount, /tmp is a small tmpfs and saves can be large). The EXIT trap
# also covers the normal path - restore happens exactly once, always.
restore_saved() {
    if [ -d "${DIR}/.Saved_keep" ]; then
        rm -rf "${DIR}/Pal/Saved"
        mv "${DIR}/.Saved_keep" "${DIR}/Pal/Saved"
    fi
}
trap restore_saved EXIT
if [ -d "${DIR}/Pal/Saved" ]; then
    echo "[update] existing save data found - preserving Pal/Saved ..."
    rm -rf "${DIR}/.Saved_keep"
    mv "${DIR}/Pal/Saved" "${DIR}/.Saved_keep"
fi

# Extract /pal/Package from every layer, in order (later layers win).
# Base OS layers simply contain no server files and are skipped.
BLOB="${DIR}/.layer.tmp"
for digest in ${LAYERS}; do
    LAYER_MB=$("${JQ}" -r --arg d "${digest}" '[.layers[] | select(.digest == $d).size][0] / 1048576 | floor' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
    echo "[update] downloading layer ${digest} (${LAYER_MB} MB) ..."
    rm -f "${BLOB}"
    curl -fsSL --http1.1 --retry 5 --retry-all-errors --retry-delay 3 -C - -o "${BLOB}" -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/blobs/${digest}" &
    CURL_PID=$!
    while kill -0 "${CURL_PID}" 2> /dev/null; do
        sleep 2
        if [ -f "${BLOB}" ] && kill -0 "${CURL_PID}" 2> /dev/null; then
            echo "  ... $(du -m "${BLOB}" | cut -f1) / ${LAYER_MB} MB downloaded"
        fi
    done
    wait "${CURL_PID}" || { echo "[update] ERROR: download of layer ${digest} failed."; exit 1; }
    echo "  download finished, extracting (the game data layer takes a few minutes) ..."
    tar -xzf "${BLOB}" -C "${DIR}" --strip-components=2 --wildcards 'pal/Package/*' 2> /dev/null \
        && echo "  -> server files extracted" \
        || echo "  -> no server files in this layer, skipped"
    rm -f "${BLOB}"
done

if [ ! -f "${DIR}/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping" ]; then
    echo "[update] ERROR: PalServer-Linux-Shipping missing after extraction."
    exit 1
fi

# Steamworks SDK library where the server binary looks for it (HOME=/home/container at runtime)
mkdir -p "${DIR}/.steam/sdk64"
cp -f "${DIR}/linux64/steamclient.so" "${DIR}/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so"

# Config parser applies panel variables (RCON, description, crossplay, ...)
# to PalWorldSettings.ini on every start. Best effort: never clobber the
# working copy with a failed download.
echo "[update] refreshing PalworldServerConfigParser ..."
if curl -fsSL --retry 3 -o "${DIR}/.parser.tmp" https://github.com/pelican-eggs/Palworld-Config-Parser-Tool/releases/latest/download/PalworldServerConfigParser-linux-amd64; then
    mv "${DIR}/.parser.tmp" "${DIR}/PalworldServerConfigParser"
else
    rm -f "${DIR}/.parser.tmp"
    echo "[update] parser download failed - keeping the current one"
fi

chmod +x "${DIR}/PalworldServerConfigParser" "${DIR}/PalServer.sh" "${DIR}/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping" 2> /dev/null || true

echo "${REMOTE_DIGEST}" > "${DIGEST_FILE}"
echo "[update] done - server files now match image '${TAG}'"
UPDATE_EOF
chmod +x update.sh

FORCE=1 bash ./update.sh

# Seed the config file on first install
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
    echo "Creating PalWorldSettings.ini from defaults ..."
    mkdir -p /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer
    if [ -f /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
        cp /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini
    else
        printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameWorldSettings]\nOptionSettings=()\n' > /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini
    fi
fi

echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Installation completed (server files from the official image)"
echo "Updates: automatic at startup while 'Auto Update' is enabled;"
echo "'Reinstall' in the panel always force-refreshes."
echo "-----------------------------------------"

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The name of the server shown in the server browser. The characters " ( ) are not allowed - they corrupt the config parser.

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: The description of the server. The characters " ( ) are not allowed - they corrupt the config parser.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players allowed on the server (1-32).

variables.name: Server Password
variables.description: If specified, players must provide this password to join the server.

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description:
Password for administrator commands (in-game chat and RCON). Required - it is also used by
the panel console and the stop command, which are forwarded to the server via RCON.

variables.name: Community Server
variables.description: Set to 1 to register the server in the community server browser (-publiclobby). Leave at 0 for a private/invite (IP + port) server.

variables.name: Public IP
variables.description: Set this to the server's public IP address. Only needed if the allocation is a local IP, otherwise the allocated IP is used automatically.

variables.name: Crossplay Platforms
variables.description:
Comma-separated list of platforms allowed to connect. Valid values: Steam, Xbox, PS5, Mac.
Example: Steam,Xbox,PS5,Mac

variables.name: RCON Port
variables.description: Internal RCON port used by the panel console. Does not need to be allocated.

variables.name: Enable RCON
variables.description: Must be True - the panel console and stop command depend on it.

variables.name: Performance Threading Flags
variables.description:
Set to 1 to add the legacy -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS flags.
The official docs state these may be unnecessary or even harmful on v1.0+ — leave at 0 unless
testing shows they help on your hardware.

variables.name: Image Tag (Server Version)
variables.description:
Tag of the official Docker image (ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver) to install from, e.g.
"latest" or "v1.0.1.100619". Applied when the server is (re)installed.

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description:
Update the server files from the official Docker image during startup whenever Pocketpair
publishes a new version (uses the Image Tag variable; the check costs about a second when
nothing changed). Disable to pin the currently installed version - the panel's Reinstall
button always force-updates. World data is preserved either way.

variables.name: Enable REST API
variables.description:
Enables the official HTTP REST API (players, kick/ban, metrics, announce) used by the
panel's Palworld Admin plugin. Applied to PalWorldSettings.ini by the config parser on
every start. The REST API port must be allocated to the server and should be firewalled
to the panel host only - it must NEVER be reachable from the public internet.

variables.name: REST API Port
variables.description: Port for the official REST API. Needs a matching allocation on the server (TCP). Used by the Palworld Admin panel plugin.
